elements.title: Nelly Korda
elements.contents: ... that Nelly Korda (pictured), her sister, her brother, and her father have all won sporting tournaments in Australia?

elements.title: a monster from The Last of Us
elements.contents: ... that one scholar called a monster from The Last of Us a "metaphorical representation of patriarchal power"?

elements.title: Belinda Rathbone
elements.contents: ... that Belinda Rathbone and Eugenia Parry Janis collaborated on an art exhibition made up entirely of Polaroid pictures?

elements.title: Travelling People
elements.contents: ... that parts of the novel Travelling People are written as a letter, a newspaper article and a film script?

elements.title: Michael-Christopher Koji Fox
elements.contents: ... that Michael-Christopher Koji Fox became the lead vocalist for a rock band after translating and writing lyrics for Final Fantasy XIV?

elements.title: D-Day
elements.contents: ... that, according to D-Day, the US Army treated 30,000 cases of combat exhaustion during the Battle of Normandy?

elements.title: Coulterville Main Street Historic District
elements.contents: ... that the architecture of Coulterville Main Street Historic District, rebuilt after a series of major fires, is a layering of different techniques and materials?

elements.title: Grete Scherzer
elements.contents: ... that the reasons why pianist Grete Scherzer permanently ended her career after a 1957 concert remain a "mystery"?

elements.title: Pieniny Castle
elements.contents: ... that Pieniny Castle, invisible from most nearby locations, was "masterfully integrated into the natural landscape"?
